volumeTitle: Children of Heracles. Hippolytus. Andromache. Hecuba
label: Children of Heracles (Section 981 - Section 1053)

sections.label: Section 981
sections.original: δεινόν τι καὶ συγγνωστόν , ὦ γύναι , σʼ ἔχει
sections.translation: Lady , full well I understand thou hast a dire quarrel with this man , and ’ tis pardonable .

sections.label: Section 983
sections.original: γύναι , σάφʼ ἴσθι μή με θωπεύσοντά σε ,
sections.translation: Woman , be sure I will not flatter thee nor say aught to save my life ,

sections.label: Section 985
sections.original: λέξονθʼ ὅθεν χρὴ δειλίαν ὀφλεῖν τινα .
sections.translation: that can give any occasion for a charge of cowardice . It was not of my own free will I took this quarrel up ; I am aware that I was born thy cousin , and kinsman to Heracles , thy son ; but whether I would or no ,

sections.label: Section 990
sections.original: Ἥρα με κάμνειν τήνδʼ ἔθηκε τὴν νόσον .
sections.translation: Hera , by her power divine , caused me to be afflicted thus . Still , when I undertook to be his foe , and when I knew I had to enter on this struggle , I set myself to devise trouble in plenty , and oft from time to time my midnight communing bore fruit ,

sections.label: Section 995
sections.original: ὅπως διώσας καὶ κατακτείνας ἐμοὺς
sections.translation: scheming how to push aside and slay my foes , and for the future divorce myself from fear ; for I knew that son of thine was no mere cipher , but a man indeed ; yea , for , though he was my foe , I will speak well of him , because he was a man of worth .

sections.label: Section 1000
sections.original: κείνου δʼ ἀπαλλαχθέντος οὐκ ἐχρῆν μʼ ἄρα
sections.translation: Now , after he was taken hence , was I not forced , by reason of these children ’ s hatred , and because I was conscious of an hereditary feud , to leave no stone unturned by slaying , banishing , and plotting against them ? So long as I did so , my safety was assured .

sections.label: Section 1005
sections.original: οὔκουν σύ γʼ ἀναλαβοῦσα τὰς ἐμὰς τύχας
sections.translation: Suppose thyself hadst had my lot , wouldst not thou have set to harassing the lion ’ s angry whelps , instead of letting them dwell at Argos undisturbed ? Thou wilt not persuade us otherwise . Now therefore , since they did not slay me then ,

sections.label: Section 1010
sections.original: πρόθυμον ὄντα , τοῖσιν Ἑλλήνων νόμοις
sections.translation: when I was prepared to die , by the laws of Hellas my death becomes a curse on him , who slays me now . The city wisely let me go , in that she regarded the gods more than her hatred of me . Thou hast had my answer to thy words ; henceforth must

sections.label: Section 1015
sections.original: τὸν προστρόπαιον τόν τε γενναῖον καλεῖν .
sections.translation: I be called avenging spirit [*] and noble hero too . [*] ’ Tis even thus with me ; to die have I no wish , but , if I leave my life , I shall in no way be grieved .

sections.label: Section 1018
sections.original: παραινέσαι σοι σμικρόν , Ἀλκμήνη , θέλω ,
sections.translation: Alcmena , fain I would advise thee somewhat ; let this man go , for ’ tis the city ’ s will .

sections.label: Section 1020
sections.original: τί δʼ , ἢν θάνῃ τε καὶ πόλει πιθώμεθα ;
sections.translation: Suppose he die , and yet I obey the city ?

sections.label: Section 1021
sections.original: τὰ λῷστ ’ ἂν εἴη · πῶς τάδʼ οὖν γενήσεται ;
sections.translation: That would be best of all ; but how can this be ?

sections.label: Section 1022
sections.original: ἐγὼ διδάξω ῥᾳδίως · κτανοῦσα γὰρ
sections.translation: I will teach thee easily . I will slay him and then give up his corpse to those of his friends , who come for it , for , as regards his body , I will not disobey [*] the state ;

sections.label: Section 1025
sections.original: οὗτος δὲ δώσει τὴν δίκην θανὼν ἐμοί .
sections.translation: but by his death shall he pay me the penalty .

sections.label: Section 1026
sections.original: κτεῖνʼ , οὐ παραιτοῦμαί σε · τήνδε δὲ πτόλιν ,
sections.translation: Slay me , I do not ask thee for mercy ; yet since this city let me go and shrunk from slaying me , I will reward it with an old oracle of Loxias , which in time will benefit them more than doth appear .

sections.label: Section 1030
sections.original: θανόντα γάρ με θάψεθʼ οὗ τὸ μόρσιμον ,
sections.translation: Bury my body after death in its destined grave in front of the shrine of the virgin goddess [*] at Pallene . And I will be thy friend and guardian of thy city for ever , where I lie buried in a foreign soil , but a bitter foe to these children ’ s descendants ,

sections.label: Section 1035
sections.original: ὅταν μόλωσι δεῦρο σὺν πολλῇ χερὶ
sections.translation: whensoe ’ er [*] with gathered host they come against this land , traitors to your kindness now ; such are the strangers ye have championed . Why then came I hither , if I knew all this , instead of regarding the god ’ s oracle ? Because I thought , that Hera was mightier far than any oracle ,

sections.label: Section 1040
sections.original: κοὐκ ἂν προδοῦναί μʼ . ἀλλὰ μήτε μοι χοὰς
sections.translation: and would not betray me . Waste no drink-offering on my tomb , nor spill the victim ’ s blood ; for I will requite them for my treatment here with a journey they shall rue ; and ye shall have double gain from me , for I will help you and harm them by my death .

sections.label: Section 1045
sections.original: τί δῆτα μέλλετʼ , εἰ πόλει σωτηρίαν
sections.translation: Why , why delay to kill this man , after hearing this , since this is needed to secure the safety of your city and your children ? Himself points out the safest road . Though the man is now our foe , yet after death is he our gain .

sections.label: Section 1050
sections.original: κομίζετʼ αὐτόν , δμῶες · εἶτα χρὴ κυσὶν
sections.translation: Away with him , ye servants , and cast him [*] to the dogs when ye have slain him . Think not thou shalt live to cast me forth from my native land again .

sections.label: Section 1053
sections.original: ταὐτὰ δοκεῖ μοι . στείχετʼ , ὀπαδοί .
sections.translation: I agree . Lead on , servants . Our [*] conduct shall bring no stain of guilt upon our rulers .
